Your Path to an Electric Home, Simplified.

Power your life smarter and further.

The electric home manual you never got, written for the average owner.

Your path to an electric home, simplified. Connecting your EV, solar, and battery storage into one efficient system. From home chargers and solar gear to cordless tool batteries and EV road trips — learn it all in one place.

Try the Sim-Lab Browse the guides
● Featured guide · EV Home Charging

Level 1 vs Level 2 Home Charging: The 2026 Comparison Guide

Which home charger actually fits your driving? Compare real charging speeds, install cost, and cost-per-mile side by side — the clear way to settle the Level 1 vs Level 2 question.

Range added per hour of charging
Level 1 · 120V 3–5 mi/hr
Level 2 · 240V ~30 mi/hr
DC Fast 200+ mi / 30 min
Read the full comparison →
Try it right now — free

What does home charging really cost you?

Most drivers overestimate it. Type your battery size, your electric rate, and your monthly miles — the answer updates instantly. Then share your number.

  • Home charging typically runs 3–5¢ per mile — a third of gasoline.
  • Overnight time-of-use rates can cut that nearly in half again.
  • Level 1 vs Level 2 costs the same per kWh — the difference is speed.
Calculator

EV Home Charging Cost

Estimate what a full charge and your monthly charging costs at home.

$— Full charge (0→100%)
$— Est. monthly cost
—¢ Cost per mile

Estimates only, based on the numbers you enter. Actual utility rates, fees, and charging losses vary.

The numbers that matter

National Energy Benchmarks

17.9¢per kWh Avg. U.S. home electricity rate National residential average, 2026 ⓘ U.S. EIA — Electric Power Monthly, Table 5.6.A View source ↗
~80% of EV charging happens at home Overnight Level 2 is the norm ⓘ U.S. DOE — Alternative Fuels Data Center View source ↗
3–5¢per mile Typical home charging cost Roughly a third of gasoline ⓘ U.S. DOE — eGallon / FuelEconomy.gov View source ↗
~30mi/hr Range added on Level 2 (240V) vs. 3–5 mi/hr on Level 1 ⓘ U.S. DOT — Rural EV Toolkit (charging speeds) View source ↗
20min DC fast charge to 80% At high-power public stations ⓘ U.S. DOT — Rural EV Toolkit (charging speeds) View source ↗
37miles Avg. miles driven per day (U.S.) Well within a nightly charge ⓘ U.S. DOT FHWA — National Household Travel Survey View source ↗
4–5hrs/day Avg. U.S. peak sun hours What sizes a solar array ⓘ NREL — PVWatts / NSRDB solar resource data View source ↗
9.6kW Top EV onboard power output Run a campsite or your home ⓘ Ford — F-150 Lightning Pro Power Onboard spec View source ↗
The Electric Home Manual

Owning an electric home shouldn't be a DIY research project. We curate the complex technical data into plain-English guides and interactive simulators, helping you put your energy assets to work—saving you money, reducing your footprint, and making you the expert for your family and friends.

Hover or tap an asset to see it light up

Where Your Power Goes

A typical EV home's monthly usage

EV charging 32%
Heating & cooling 24%
Water heater 13%
Kitchen & laundry 12%
Lighting & electronics 10%
Phantom loads 9%
Illustrative — based on a typical ~900 kWh/mo EV home. Hover a bar for kWh.
See what a monitor catches that your bill hides →
EVs Ready for Adventure

Best EVs for camping (V2L power)

Vehicle V2L Battery Range From
Ford F-150 Lightning★ 9.6 kW ~131 kWh ~320 mi ~$63k
Chevrolet Silverado EV 7.2 kW ~205 kWh ~450 mi ~$75k
Kia EV9 3.6 kW ~100 kWh ~304 mi ~$56k
Hyundai Ioniq 5★ 3.6 kW ~84 kWh ~318 mi ~$43k
Kia EV6 3.6 kW ~84 kWh ~319 mi ~$43k
Rivian R1T 1.5 kW ~141 kWh ~420 mi ~$70k
★ Top camping pick · figures approximate, vary by trim & year See the full EV camping breakdown →

Latest guides

EV Home Charging

Apartment and Condo EV Charging: What's Actually Possible

No garage, no driveway, shared parking — and still very chargeable. The realistic paths to EV charging for apartment and condo dwellers, ranked by effort.
EV Home Charging

Choosing a Charger With the Right Cable Length for Your Driveway

Cable length is the charger spec people regret most. How to measure your real reach, why longer usually wins, and the habits that make any length work.
EV Home Charging

How Far Can Your Charger Be From the Electrical Panel?

There's no hard distance limit for EV charger circuits — but distance drives wire size, voltage drop, and cost. How far is practical, and what long runs need.
EV Home Charging

What a Charger's NEMA Rating Means for Weather Resistance

NEMA 3R, NEMA 4, NEMA 4X — the enclosure codes on charger spec sheets, decoded. What each rating protects against and which one your installation needs.
EV Home Charging

Best Home Charger Placement for a Two-Car Garage

Where you mount the charger in a two-car garage decides which cars it reaches and how annoying daily use is. The placement logic, port maps, and cable math.
EV Home Charging

How Much Charging Adds to Your Bill If You Work From Home

Working from home means less driving and more daytime charging temptation. The real bill math for WFH EV owners, and the one habit that keeps costs low.
